title: Special Topics-Political Science
catalogDescription: An in-depth study of specific topics in political science or direct involvement in a politically-oriented project. May be in a seminar format or be research focused.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 and instructor consent for individual research projects.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: American Political Participation
catalogDescription: Considers the causes and consequences of various forms of American political participation. Focuses on elections, social movements, and political violence while considering representation and citizenship. Through examination of qualitative and quantitative methods, students will investigate democratic theories in historical and current political discourse.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: State and Local Government Politics
catalogDescription: Explores and analyzes histories, structures, processes, policies, administration, and politics of state and local governments. Washington state receives close attention. Explores interest groups, lobbying, campaign finance, electoral politics, policy development and implementation, legislatures, executives, judiciary systems, ballot measures, political personalities, and intergovernmental relations. Students engage current political issues.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: The Arab-Israeli Conflict
catalogDescription: Explores the Arab-Israeli conflict from its inception until the present. Examines key political processes in the development and persistence of the conflict. Investigates the conflict while applying key political science concepts including: state and institution building, inequality, human rights, ethnic conflict, nationalism, development, and conflict management.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Immigration, Refugees, and Citizenship
catalogDescription: Immigration, refugee, and citizenship policies are hot button topics across the world. This class introduces students to key debates, research, and theories around these issues. We will explore why people immigrate, immigration and asylum policies, anti-immigrant sentiment and groups, undocumented immigration and border security, migrant inclusion and exclusion, and the complexities around defining citizenship and belonging in host societies.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.

title: Introduction to Political Science
catalogDescription: Explores politics as systems of ideologies, processes, and institutions. Offers an overview of basic political science concepts, theories, and methods and introduces central political issues and debates. As a survey course, it provides a broad overview of the discipline and key political science approaches to studying politics.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 or instructor consent.

title: Introduction to Law: United States
catalogDescription: Introduction and overview of the legal system and processes in the United States through an examination of several substantive areas of law. Includes an introduction to legal procedure, business ethics, jurisdiction, Constitutional law, criminal and civil law, torts and negligence, product liability, and property. Provides an overview of the Courts and looks at law as a framework in which claims are resolved. Uses a case method approach which utilizes a critical thinking perspective.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL 99 .
Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for READ 104 .

title: Introduction to Political Theory
catalogDescription: Provides an introduction to the study and nature of politics. This course explores key political concepts, such as liberty, equality, justice, authority, power, and identity. Political theory engages with the foundations of political community and institutions as a way to encourage a responsible and politically active citizen.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.

title: United States Government
catalogDescription: Introduces students to the study of the United States political system. Explores United States political development, key political actors, formal and informal political institutions, means of citizen participation, and the analysis of political processes and outcomes. Examines key theories, concepts, and approaches for studying of United States Politics.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.

title: International Relations
catalogDescription: A broad overview of international relations. Explores key theoretical approaches and concepts for studying international politics. Examines a host of central issues in international relations including: the evolution of the state system, international cooperation and conflict, international political economy, and international institutions and organizations.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.

title: Comparative Government
catalogDescription: A comparative analysis of politics and types of governing systems around the globe (e.g., democratic, authoritarian, theocratic, parliamentary, presidential). Examines power relationships (e.g., race, ethnicity, religion, sex) and citizen rights. Explores and analyzes the political issues and problems in their international, historical, economic, and cultural contexts.
prerequisiteNotes: Official Green River enrollment requirement text: Eligible for ENGL& 101 or instructor consent.
requirement text is preserved as a note until a parser can safely normalize AND/OR/instructor-consent semantics into graph prerequisites.
